channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: US push for unified Libyan government tests Tripoli factions
item.description: A US-backed initiative aiming to unify Libya's divided institutions tests political loyalties in the country's west.

item.title: Why Britain devours its prime ministers
item.description: Weak leaders are only part of the story. Restless MPs and volatile voters have made Downing Street harder to survive.

item.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian man during raid on his home
item.description: Israeli forces kill Palestinian man during raid on his home

item.title: How minesweeping in the Strait of Hormuz works: A visual guide
item.description: Clearing mines is a slow, high-risk operation that involves finding and destroying them using specialised techniques.

item.title: Watch World Cup Day 14: South Africa advance, with Morocco, Mexico, Brazil
item.description: Latest match highlights, reaction and previews from the FIFA World Cup 2026 in North America.

item.title: Rubio tells Gulf allies that Iran deal will ensure their security
item.description: Rubio's Gulf tour is the first high-level diplomatic mission since the US-Iran framework agreement last week.

item.title: Europe faces another day of extreme heat after more temperature records set
item.description: France under extreme heat alert as 63 million endure 30+C temperatures, with widespread power outages in Brittany.

item.title: As Ukraine seizes ‘first chance to win’, war horrors come home to Russia
item.description: President Putin has hinted at peace talks as the economy fails, people flee cities and toxic 'oil rains' fall.

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Why is Central America so vulnerable to tremors?
item.description: Venezuela and Japan are both hit by strong quakes, but Venezuela faces higher risks due to weaker building standards.

item.title: Oil prices back to pre-war levels on rising Middle East supply
item.description: The price of Brent crude has reached its lowest since February 27, before the war started.

item.title: US says Iran will buy its goods: What could US-Iran trade look like?
item.description: Before the 1979 revolution in Iran, Washington and Tehran had close trade ties. Could a peace deal revive them?

item.title: In pictures: Aftermath of twin earthquakes in Venezuela
item.description: Communities in northern Venezuela struggle after deadly quakes as rescuers race against time to find survivors.

item.title: World reacts as devastating Venezuela earthquakes kill at least 164
item.description: Global leaders express solidarity with Venezuela as dual earthquakes kill at least 164 and leave widespread devastation.

item.title: Which teams have qualified for the World Cup 2026 knockouts, round of 32?
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout stage format, criteria and rules for qualification. Find out who's in and who's out.

item.title: Iran warns against Hormuz crossings without authorisation
item.description: An oil tanker navigates the strait, reportedly hugging the western coast, despite threats from Revolutionary Guards.

item.title: Magnitude 6.9 earthquake strikes northern Japan
item.description: Magnitude 6.9 earthquake strikes northern Japan

item.title: Moments of panic as earthquakes strike Venezuela
item.description: Video captured the first moments of two earthquakes striking Venezuela, triggering panic as people fled for safety.

item.title: Mexico fans celebrate three World Cup group-stage wins in a row
item.description: Mexico fans celebrate three World Cup group-stage wins in a row

item.title: Iran war day 118: IRGC rejects new Hormuz route; Rubio to meet GCC leaders
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard warns against alternative route in Strait of Hormuz as top US diplomat to meet Gulf leaders.

item.title: South Africa fans hail historic World Cup knockout entry after Korea win
item.description: The nation’s shock win against South Korea quelled criticism back at home after doubts about coach Hugo Broos’ team.

item.title: South Africa celebrates reaching World Cup knockout stage for first time
item.description: South Africa’s team has celebrated reaching the World Cup knockout stage for the first time after defeating South Korea.

item.title: ICC judges sue Trump over sanctions, call measures unlawful
item.description: Judges argue sanctions aimed to punish and coerce ICC decisions on US and Israeli war crimes cases.

item.title: ‘We hugged each other and ran’: Venezuelans recount earthquake horror
item.description: Survivors describe panic and collapsing buildings during two strong quakes as thousands of people feared dead.

item.title: In New York’s Astoria neighbourhood, Arab nations unite in World Cup joy
item.description: Historic World Cup match wins for Egypt, Morocco and Algeria bring together fans from the Middle Eastern diaspora.

item.title: Rescuers pull survivors from rubble after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: A major search-and-rescue effort is underway in Venezuela after two earthquakes levelled buildings and left many trapped
